interviewStages: Behavioral: customer focus, mission-driven (deep reading), calm pace
codingTopics: Feeds and ranking: heaps and top-K over content items
Search and autocomplete: prefix tries, typeahead
Streaming: rolling engagement metrics over user activity events
Caching: LRU caches for content items
systemDesignTopics: Design a recommendation system for personalized reading feeds
Design a rich-text editor with images, code, and collaboration
Design content quality detection (paywall vs free, spam, AI-generated)

coding: At Medium, given a stream of user activity events, return the top-K trending content items in the last hour using a heap. Discuss memory bounds.
Build autocomplete over content items titles using a prefix trie and return the top suggestions for a typed prefix.
Rank a personalized feed of content items for a user from their history. Discuss the scoring approach.
Implement an LRU cache for content items with a size cap. Discuss eviction and complexity.
Given a stream of user activity events, compute per-user daily streaks with correct time-zone handling.
behavioral: Tell me about a project you are proud of at Medium scale and the hardest tradeoff you made.
Give me an example that shows customer focus, mission-driven (deep reading), calm pace.
Describe a time you disagreed with a teammate or manager. How did it resolve?
Tell me about a time you took ownership of an ambiguous problem end to end.
systemDesign: Design a recommendation system for personalized reading feeds.
Design a rich-text editor with images, code, and collaboration.
Design content quality detection (paywall vs free, spam, AI-generated).
values: Medium serves a huge consumer audience. Describe a time you prioritized user wellbeing over an engagement metric.
Tell me about a time you owned a consumer-facing reliability problem.
How do you balance business goals against a healthy user experience?
other: How does Medium compare to Substack?

rawSummary:
Medium Interview Guide (2026): Publishing Platform Engineering

				techinterview.org			
			
		

		 /  May 5, 2026 
			
		

	
	
	

		
		
On this page

- Process

- What they actually ask

- Levels and comp (2026)

- Prep priorities

Updated May 5, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Medium
medium.com ↗

Medium is one of the largest long-form publishing platforms — 100M+ monthly visitors, paid membership model. The interview emphasizes content recommendation, editor experience, and the realities of running a creator-economy platform.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding phone (DSA medium) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 3–4 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design a recommendation system for personalized reading feeds

- Design a rich-text editor with images, code, and collaboration

- Design content quality detection (paywall vs free, spam, AI-generated)

- Coding: medium DSA, often with text-processing or graph framing

- Behavioral: customer focus, mission-driven (deep reading), calm pace

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $150K–$185K total

- Senior SE: $215K–$285K

- Staff: $310K–$410K

- Principal: $430K–$570K

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in TypeScript/Node.js (the bulk of the codebase)

- Understand recommendation systems and content engagement metrics

- Brush up on rich-text editor patterns (ProseMirror, Slate)

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Medium remote-friendly?

Distributed-first since 2020. Hubs in San Francisco and NYC.

How does Medium compare to Substack?

Medium is curated platform with paid membership. Substack is creator-owned newsletters. Different models. Medium pays mid-tier; below FAANG.

What is the engineering culture?

Mission-driven, calm pace, mature. Strong work-life balance. Less expansive than peak years; reliable.
